name: azure-appconfiguration-py
description: Azure App Configuration SDK for Python skill for centralized configuration management, feature flags, and dynamic settings with Claude Code.
capabilities: Connect to Azure App Configuration using connection strings or Entra ID authentication
Read, create, update, and delete configuration settings with key-value pairs
Manage feature flags for gradual rollouts and A/B testing
Use labels for environment-specific configuration (dev, staging, production)
Create snapshots for point-in-time configuration rollback
Work with both sync and async Python clients
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill consisting of documentation markdown only. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code and found no security issues. No dangerous patterns, no risk factors detected, and no prompt injection attempts found.
valueStatement: Teams need centralized configuration management across environments. This skill provides Claude Code with the Azure App Configuration SDK for Python to read, write, and manage configuration settings and feature flags programmatically.
userTitle: Manage Azure App Configuration with Python
limitations: Requires valid Azure subscription and App Configuration store
Cannot create new App Configuration stores programmatically
Requires network access to Azure endpoints

useCases.title: Manage Configuration Across Environments
useCases.description: Use labels to organize settings for development, staging, and production environments with a single codebase.

useCases.title: Implement Feature Flags
useCases.description: Create and manage feature flags to enable gradual rollouts and A/B testing without deploying new code.

useCases.title: Centralized Configuration
useCases.description: Store all application configuration in one place for easier management and version control.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to connect to Azure App Configuration using Python with a connection string.

promptTemplates.title: Read Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I read a configuration setting by key in Azure App Configuration using Python?

promptTemplates.title: Feature Flags
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a feature flag in Azure App Configuration and explain how to check if it's enabled.

promptTemplates.title: Environment Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use labels to manage configuration for different environments like development, staging, and production.

bestPractices: Use Entra ID authentication instead of connection strings in production for better security
Use labels to separate configurations across environments (dev, staging, production)
Create snapshots before making bulk configuration changes for easy rollback
antiPatterns: Storing connection strings in source code instead of environment variables
Hardcoding configuration values instead of using Azure App Configuration
Not using labels when managing multiple environments leads to configuration conflicts

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure App Configuration?
faq.answer: You can use either a connection string or Entra ID authentication. Entra ID is recommended for production as it avoids storing secrets.

faq.question: Can I manage feature flags with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports creating, reading, updating, and deleting feature flags in Azure App Configuration.

faq.question: How do I handle different environments?
faq.answer: Use labels to organize configuration by environment like development, staging, and production.

faq.question: Does this support async operations?
faq.answer: Yes, there is an async client available (azure.appconfiguration.aio) for use with asyncio in Python.

faq.question: Can I export my configuration?
faq.answer: Yes, use snapshots to create point-in-time exports of your configuration settings for backup or rollback.

faq.question: Is this skill secure to use?
faq.answer: The skill is safe to use. For production, use Entra ID authentication and store credentials securely in Azure Key Vault.
